Operation 'scrape' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, missing_output_details. Original summary 'Non-JS scraping' is too technical and vague for AI agents. Description lacks clarity about when to use this endpoint, what it returns, and its capabilities. Added context about use cases, return values, and key features like proxy rotation and retries to improve AI agent interpretability and help agents decide between /scrape and /scrape-js endpoints.

data.current_description: Use high-performance web scraping endpoint with Chrome browser TLS fingerprint, but without JavaScript execution and real browser overhead.
data.summary_suggestion: Scrape web content with high-performance engine
data.description_suggestion: Scrapes web content using a high-performance engine with Chrome browser TLS fingerprint. Does not execute JavaScript or render CSS, making it faster and lighter than browser-based scraping. Ideal for static HTML content and when JavaScript execution is not required. Returns the scraped HTML content and metadata. Supports proxy rotation and smart retries.
data.reasoning: Original summary 'Non-JS scraping' is too technical and vague for AI agents. Description lacks clarity about when to use this endpoint, what it returns, and its capabilities. Added context about use cases, return values, and key features like proxy rotation and retries to improve AI agent interpretability and help agents decide between /scrape and /scrape-js endpoints.

message: Operation 'scrape-js' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_context, missing_output_details, not_actionable. Original summary lacks clarity about the JavaScript capability. Description is too vague ('use only when features are not enough') and doesn't explain what the endpoint returns or its trade-offs. Added specific details about JavaScript/CSS support, return values, performance characteristics, and when to use this vs. /scrape to improve AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Launches real Chrome browser engine, use only when /scrape endpoint features are not enough.
data.summary_suggestion: Scrape web content with JavaScript rendering
data.description_suggestion: Scrapes web content using a real Chrome browser engine with full JavaScript and CSS evaluation. Use this endpoint when the target website requires JavaScript execution or dynamic content rendering. Supports screenshots, cookies, and complex interactions. Returns the rendered HTML content, metadata, and optionally screenshots. Higher latency and resource usage compared to /scrape endpoint. Supports proxy rotation and smart retries.
data.reasoning: Original summary lacks clarity about the JavaScript capability. Description is too vague ('use only when features are not enough') and doesn't explain what the endpoint returns or its trade-offs. Added specific details about JavaScript/CSS support, return values, performance characteristics, and when to use this vs. /scrape to improve AI agent decision-making.

data.current_description: Launches real Chrome browser engine with better success rate for websites protected by Cloudflare, Datadome, Kasada, PermimeterX. Only available via APIRoad.
data.description_suggestion: Scrapes web content using an enhanced real Chrome browser engine optimized for websites protected by anti-bot services (Cloudflare, Datadome, Kasada, PerimeterX). Provides improved success rates against bot detection systems compared to /scrape-js. Includes full JavaScript and CSS evaluation, screenshots, and cookie support. Returns rendered HTML content, metadata, and optionally screenshots. Only available via APIRoad. Higher latency and resource usage. Supports proxy rotation and smart retries.
data.reasoning: Summary is adequate but description lacks important context about availability restrictions, return values, and how this differs from /scrape-js.
